key_points: The Earth is made up of several concentric layers, much like an onion.
The three main layers of the Earth are the Crust, the Mantle, and the Core.
The Crust is the uppermost and thinnest layer of the Earth's surface.
The Earth's crust is about 35 km on the continental masses and only 5 km on the ocean floors.
The Earth is constantly undergoing changes both inside and outside.
examples: Continental Crust: Made of Silica and Alumina (Sial).
Oceanic Crust: Made of Silica and Magnesium (Sima).
Deepest Mine: Located in South Africa (about 4 km deep).
exam_tips: Remember that the Crust is the thinnest of all the layers, making up less than 1% of the Earth's volume.
The Mantle extends up to a depth of 2900 km below the crust.
The innermost layer is the Core, with a radius of about 3500 km.
Understand that the central core has very high temperature and pressure.
